title: Navigation History, Back Semantics, Snapshots, and Failure Recovery · Systems Transfer
summary: An independent conservation group receives a different region, command sequence, API fixture, cache age, concurrency budget, accessibility need, platform, and injected failure and transfers navigation history, back semantics, snapshots, and failure recovery into a changed bounded snapshot navigation history. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of bounded snapshot navigation history with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit…
objectives: Define whether back means prior successful view, API previous link, or numeric page and expose that contract in help.
Store immutable-enough page snapshots or identifiers with explicit copy boundaries so later mutation cannot rewrite history.
Push history only after a new view is admitted and leave cursor plus history unchanged on failure or cancellation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

lab case — A lab Go team handles case 8395 during a race-instrumented cache stress case. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate bounded successful-view snapshots and failure-safe back behavior; no user-visible meaning may rely on color or cursor motion; then change one catch, owned record, encounter relation, or history state and prove the prior invariant. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Generate navigation sequences and verify cursor, stack, boundary, idempotence, and failure invariants across changed page sizes.

One precondition fails while output still looks plausible. What is the correct response?

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Generate navigation sequences and verify cursor, stack, boundary, idempotence, and failure invariants across changed page sizes.
